body:
This PR adds an implicit free surface solver for `NonhydrostaticModel`. This will be useful for large eddy simulations of internal tide generation, breaking, and turbulent mixing. Work with @shriyafruitwala

What we did so far:

- [x] added `free_surface` property to `NonhydrostaticModel`
- [x] added an `InhomogeneousFormulation` option for the tridiagonal formulation of `FourierTridiagonalPoissonSolver`
- [x] started work to modify `solve_for_pressure!` and `pressure_correction!`

TODO:

- [x] write code to compute the inhomogeneous contribution to the source term of `FourierTridiagonalPoissonSolver`
- [x] write code to modify the main diagonal of the solver prior to calling `solve!(presure, solver)` in `solve_for_pressure!`
- [x] clarify "which dz" enter into RHS + diagonal contributions
- [x] add MixedBoundaryCondition to pressure + modify `fill_halo_regions!(pressure)` so that the pressure gradient across the top boundary is correct
- [x] add a routine to update the free surface following the pressure solve
- [ ] reorganize code so that `materialize_free_surface` and `ImplicitFreeSurface` are available in the `NonhydrostaticModels` module (precisely how to do this is a matter of discussion)
- [x] Compute the tendency and update `w*` at `k = Nz + 1` when there is non-zero vertical velocity tendency at the surface

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: Implicit free surface formulation for NonhydrostaticModel
